questions.question:

The number of 8-bit strings beginning with either 111 or 101 is

Given the following statements:

  1. Recursive enumerable sets are closed under complementation.
  2. Recursive sets are closed under complementation.

Which is/are the correct statement?



UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 57

Which of the following regular expression identifies are true?



UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 45

Which is not the correct statement(s)?

  1. Every context sensitive language is recursive
  2. There is a recursive language that is not context sensitive


UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 25, UGCNET-Dec2013-III: 42
options: I is true and II is true
I is false and II is true
questions.correct_answer: I is true and II is true

UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 50

Given a Non-deterministic Finite Automaton (NFA) with states \( \text{p} \) and \( \text{r} \) as initial and final states respectively and transition table as given below \( : \)

\[ \begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|} \hline \text{}  & \text{a} & \text{b} \\\hline \text{p} & \text{-} & \text{q} \\\hline \text{q} & \text{r} & \text{s} \\\hline \text{r} & \text{r} & \text{s} \\\hline \text{s} & \text{r}  & \text{s} \\\hline\end{array} \]

The minimum number of states required in Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA) equivalent to NFA is



UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 2 | Question: 24

The grammar with production rules \( S \rightarrow aSb \mid SS \mid \lambda \) generates language \( L \) given by:



UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 36
options: \( L = \{ w \in \{a, b\}* \mid n_a(w) = n_b(w) \text{and} n_a(v) \geq n_b(v) \text{where v is any prefix of w} \} \)
\( L = \{ w \in \{a, b\}* \mid n_a(w) = n_b(w) \text{and} n_a(v) \leq n_b(v) \text{where v is any prefix of w} \} \)
\( L = \{ w \in \{a, b\}* \mid n_a(w) \neq n_b(w) \text{and} n_a(v) \geq n_b(v) \text{where v is any prefix of w} \} \)
\( L = \{ w \in \{a, b\}* \mid n_a(w) \neq n_b(w) \text{and} n_a(v) \leq n_b(v) \text{where v is any prefix of w} \} \)
questions.correct_answer: \( L = \{ w \in \{a, b\}* \mid n_a(w) = n_b(w) \text{and} n_a(v) \geq n_b(v) \text{where v is any prefix of w} \} \)

questions.question:

Codes:



UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 39

The statements s1 and s2 are given as:

s1: Context sensitive languages are closed under intersection, concatenation, substitution and inverse homomorphism.

s2: Context sensitive languages are closed under concatenation, substitution and homomorphism.

Which of the following is correct statement?



UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 40
options: s1 is correct s2 is not correct
s1 is not correct s2 is correct
Both s1 and s2 are not correct
questions.correct_answer: s1 is correct s2 is not correct

questions.question:
Given the following statements:

\( S_1: \text{The grammars}S \rightarrow asb \mid bsa \mid ss \mid a \text{and} S \rightarrow asb \mid bsa \mid a \) are not equivalent.

\( S_2: \text{The grammars}S \rightarrow ss \mid sss \mid asb \mid bsa \mid \lambda \text{and} S \rightarrow ss \mid asb \mid bsa \mid \lambda \) are equivalent.



UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 21
options: \( S_1 \) is correct and \( S_2 \) is not correct
\( S_1 \) is not correct and \( S_2 \) is correct
Both \( S_1 \) ad \( S_2 \) are not correct
questions.correct_answer: \( S_1 \) is correct and \( S_2 \) is not correct

questions.question:
The Greibach normal form grammar for the language \( L=\{a^n b^{n +1} \mid n \geq 0 \} \) is



UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 41
options: \( S \rightarrow a \, SB, B \rightarrow bB \mid b \)
\( S \rightarrow a \, SB \mid b, B \rightarrow b \)
questions.correct_answer: \( S \rightarrow a \, SB \mid b, B \rightarrow b \)

questions.question:
Given the following grammars:

\( G_1 \) \( S \rightarrow AB \mid aaB \)
  \( A \rightarrow aA \mid \epsilon \)
  \( B \rightarrow bB \mid \epsilon \)
\( G_2 \): \( S \rightarrow A \mid B \)
  \( A \rightarrow a A b \mid ab \)
  \( B \rightarrow abB \mid \epsilon \)

Which of the following is correct?



UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 63
options: \( G_1 \) is ambiguous and \( G_2 \) is unambiguous grammars
\( G_1 \) is unambiguous and \( G_2 \) is ambiguous grammars
both \( G_1 \) and \( G_2 \) are ambiguous grammars
both \( G_1 \) and \( G_2 \) are unambiguous grammars
questions.correct_answer: both \( G_1 \) and \( G_2 \) are ambiguous grammars

questions.question:
Consider Language \( A \) defined over the alphabet \( \Sigma=\{0,1\} \) as \( A=\{0^{\lfloor n/2 \rfloor} 1^n :n>=0 \} \) 

The expression \( \lfloor n/2 \rfloor \) means the floor of \( n/2 \), or what you get by rounding \( n/2 \) down to the nearest integer.

Which of the following is not an example of a string in \( A \)?



UGC NET CSE | December 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 73

According to pumping lemma for context free languages :

Let \( L \) be an infinite context free language, then there exists some positive integer m such that any \( w \in L \) with \( | w | \geq m \) can be decomposed as \( w = u v x y z \)



UGC NET CSE | December 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 63
options: With \( | vxy | \leq m \) such that \( uv^{i}xy^{i} z\in L \) for all \( i = 0, 1, 2 \)
With \( | vxy | \leq m \), and \( | vy | \geq 1 \), such that \( uv^{i}xy^{i} z\in L \) for all \( i = 0, 1, 2, ……. \)
With \( | vxy | \geq m \), and \( | vy | \leq 1 \), such that \( uv^{i}xy^{i} z\in L \) for all \( i = 0, 1, 2, ……. \)
With \( | vxy | \geq m \), and \( | vy | \geq 1 \), such that \( uv^{i}xy^{i} z\in L \) for all \( i = 0, 1, 2, ……. \)
questions.correct_answer: With \( | vxy | \leq m \), and \( | vy | \geq 1 \), such that \( uv^{i}xy^{i} z\in L \) for all \( i = 0, 1, 2, ……. \)

questions.question:
In general, in a recursive and non-recursive implementation of a problem (program):



UGC NET CSE | December 2015 | Part 2 | Question: 15
options: Both time and space complexities are better in recursive than in non-recursive program
Both time and space complexities are better in non-recursive than in recursive program
Time complexity is better in recursive version but space complexity is better in non-recursive version of a program
Space complexity is better in recursive version but time complexity is better in non-recursive version of a program
questions.correct_answer: Both time and space complexities are better in non-recursive than in recursive program

questions.question:

\( \begin{array}{clcl}   & \textbf{List – I} & & \textbf{List – II} \\   \text{(a)} & \{a^n b^n \mid n > 0\}  \text{is a deterministic} & \text{(i)} & \text{but not recursive language}\\ & \text{ context free language} \\  \text{(b)} & \text{The complement of}\{a^n b^n a^n \mid n>0\} & \text{(ii)} & \text{but not context free language}\\ & \text{is a context free language} \\  \text{(c)} & \{a^nb^na^n\}\text{is a context sensitive} & \text{(iii)} & \text{but cannot be accepted by a} \\&  \text{ language} && \text{deterministic pushdown}\\ &&& \text{automaton} \\  \text{(d)} & \text{L is a recursive language} & \text{(iv)} &\text{but not regular} \\  \end{array} \)

\( L_1 = \{ a^{n+m} b^n a^m \mid n, m \geq 0 \} \)

\( L_2 = \{ a^{n+m} b^{n+m} a^{n+m} \mid n, m \geq 0\} \)

Which of the following is correct ?

\( Code: \)



UGC NET CSE | December 2018 | Part 2 | Question: 35
options: Both \( L_1 \) and \( L_2 \) are context free languages
Both \( L_1 \) and \( L_2 \) are not context free languages
